Add your user to the 'input' group to de: Add your user to the 'input' group to detect mouse/keyboard activity:
After running the command, logout and lo: After running the command, logout and login again for changes to take effect.

Display helpful usage tips and shortcuts: Display helpful usage tips and shortcuts at the bottom of the popout.
Found a bug, want to translate, or have: Found a bug, want to translate, or have a feature request? Join us on GitHub.
The 'libinput' CLI was not found, so Aut: The 'libinput' CLI was not found, so Auto mode can't see your keyboard. Install it (Arch: libinput-tools, Debian/Ubuntu: libinput-tools, Fedora: libinput-utils) or pick a specific keyboard below.
'evtest' was not found — install it to m: 'evtest' was not found — install it to monitor a specific keyboard, or switch to Auto mode.
Your user is not in the 'input' group, s: Your user is not in the 'input' group, so keyboard events can't be read. Run: sudo usermod -aG input $USER — then log out and back in.
Paws react to mouse input: left and righ: Paws react to mouse input: left and right click hold the matching paw, other buttons slam both, scrolling drums with alternating paws.
Mouse interaction needs the 'libinput' C: Mouse interaction needs the 'libinput' CLI — install it (Arch: libinput-tools, Debian/Ubuntu: libinput-tools, Fedora: libinput-utils) or switch the keyboard to Auto mode.
Recolor the cat. Classic keeps the theme: Recolor the cat. Classic keeps the theme's default black and white, Primary follows the system accent, or pick a custom color.
